title: South Boston woman fatally stabbed boyfriend during an argument, police say
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Gisselle Pascual, 24, charged with manslaughter for allegedly stabbing boyfriend Kyle Bradford, 26, in South Boston.
Bradford found with a stab wound at 258 Old Colony Ave, died later at hospital, police reported.
Pascual's attorney claims self-defense, citing threats and past domestic assault charges against Bradford.

title: Patriots to be featured on HBO’s ‘Hard Knocks’ ahead of 2027 season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The New England Patriots will be featured on HBO's 'Hard Knocks' for the first time ahead of the 2027 season.
The Seattle Seahawks will appear on 'Hard Knocks' in 2026 after defeating the Patriots in Super Bowl LX.
NFL adjusted its 'Hard Knocks' eligibility rules, making playoff teams like the Patriots eligible for the series.

title: James Hagens notches first points with Providence Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens scored his first professional goal for the Providence Bruins during a 6-4 victory against Bridgeport Islanders.
Hagens recorded an assist in a 4-2 loss to Springfield Thunderbirds, extending his point streak to two consecutive games.
Hagens, on an AHL amateur tryout contract, may join Boston Bruins if he signs his entry-level contract this season.

title: Could Patriots circle back with Stefon Diggs? Here’s what Eliot Wolf said of a potential reunion.
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Patriots released Stefon Diggs due to his $26.8 million cap hit after one season.
Eliot Wolf indicated the Patriots might consider resigning Diggs on a cheaper deal if needed.
Diggs recorded 1,013 yards and four touchdowns in 2025 but struggled in postseason games.

title: Man sentenced for raping 2 women in Framingham store in 1989
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Stephen Paul Gale, 73, was sentenced to 50 years for raping two women in a Framingham store in 1989.
Gale was convicted of aggravated rape, kidnapping, and armed robbery after DNA evidence identified him decades later.
The Middlesex District Attorney credited 'investigative genetic genealogy' for solving the cold case in 2022.

title: Celtics’ Joe Mazzulla on possibility of winning coach of the year: ‘It’s a stupid award’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Joe Mazzulla dismisses NBA Coach of the Year award, calling it a 'stupid award' and emphasizes team contributions.
Celtics achieved fourth-best NBA record despite missing star player Jayson Tatum for most of the season.
Mazzulla prioritizes coaching staff and players' efforts over personal recognition in coaching achievements.

title: 1 dead after crash in Mattapan, police say
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: One person died in a car crash on American Legion Highway in Mattapan late Sunday night, police reported.
The crash, which occurred at 11:14 p.m., left the vehicle propped on the sidewalk.
Boston Police officers found the victim still inside the car with extensive damage to the front and driver side.

title: Should Joe Mazzulla win Coach of the Year? Here’s how he stacks up against the competition.
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Joe Mazzulla has the second-best odds for NBA Coach of the Year, behind Detroit's J.B. Bickerstaff.
Mazzulla led the Celtics to the second-best record in the Eastern Conference despite losing key players.
Mazzulla is praised for team development, turning weaknesses into strengths, and maintaining high performance.

title: Ice sculpture carved for Boston’s ‘No Kings’ rally destroyed by vandals, organizers say
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: An ice sculpture reading 'End ICE' was vandalized on Boston Common after the 'No Kings' rally.
Artist Kat Dressler designed the sculpture so damage would still convey the message against ICE.
Boston police responded to the scene, but no report was filed; rally organizers were disappointed.

title: Worcester man gets 30 years in prison for sexually exploiting minors
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Antonio Merced, 29, was sentenced to 30 years for sexually exploiting three children, including a 6-year-old girl.
Merced's 30-year federal sentence will run concurrently with his existing 25-30 year state sentence.
Prosecutors discovered Merced had been videotaping sexual abuse of minors and possessed hundreds of child pornography items.
